channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Former Georgia quarterback Carson Beck commits to play 2025 season at Miami, AP source says
item.description: CORAL GABLES, Fla. (AP) — Carson Beck is pushing his NFL plans back a year and has committed to play next season at Miami, a person with knowledge of the situation said Friday.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Lamar Jackson repeats and Ja’Marr Chase and Justin Jefferson are unanimous choices for AP All-Pro
item.description: Lamar Jackson beat out Josh Allen, and Ja’Marr Chase and Justin Jefferson are unanimous choices for The Associated Press 2024 NFL All-Pro Team. Saquon Barkley received 48 of 50 first-place votes and Patrick Surtain II got 49 from a nationwide panel of media members who regularly cover the league.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: BUCS BONUS: Tampa Bay looking for similar results in Wild Card rematch against Washington
item.description: With the Buccaneers gearing up for their fifth straight Post Season run, Barber joins a special episode of Bucs Bonus to talk about what the team needs to do to contain rookie quarterback phenom Jayden Daniels and the Washington Commanders offense that has leveled up since the two teams met in week
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: How a loss to Northern Illinois set the table for Notre Dame's run to the CFP title game
item.description: The toughest game Notre Dame will play this season is Jan. 20, the College Football Playoff national championship game against either Ohio State or Texas.
item.dc:creator: Tim Reynolds, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: UT Voice Craig Way discusses all things Longhorns ahead of playoff game against Ohio State
item.description: The Texas Longhorns are gearing up for a monumental face-off against the Ohio State Buckeyes at the College Football Playoffs semifinal.
item.dc:creator: Randy McIlvoy, KPRC 2

item.title: For Notre Dame QB Leonard, it was about the ring, not money. Now he waits for title-game opponent
item.description: Riley Leonard concedes he gave a lot of different answers when people asked him why he entered the transfer portal after last season and chose Notre Dame.
item.dc:creator: Eddie Pells, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Wives of Mallorca players say they were harassed after Spanish Super Cup game in Saudi Arabia
item.description: BARCELONA, Spain (AP) — The wives of two Mallorca players say they were harassed by local fans after a Spanish Super Cup game played in Saudi Arabia that ended in a victory for Real Madrid.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH WILSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Bay Area News Group girls athlete of the week: Nicole Steiner, Los Gatos basketball
item.description: Steiner, a senior, recorded the first triple-double in Los Gatos basketball program history, scoring 16 points, grabbing 15 rebounds and dishing 10 assists as the Wildcats beat Milpitas 61-29 to open league play.
item.dc:creator: Christian Babcock, The Mercury News

item.title: From stories to work shirts, how Jim Harbaugh's approach has turned around the Los Angeles Chargers
item.description: EL SEGUNDO, Calif. (AP) — Jim Harbaugh’s football philosophy is rooted in being physical on both sides of the ball and wearing down an opponent. However, when it comes to relating to his players, it is nothing but love, respect and adulation.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NFL moves Vikings-Rams playoff game to Arizona after days of devastating wildfires in Los Angeles
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The NFL has moved the Rams' wild-card playoff game against the Minnesota Vikings to Arizona after days of devastating wildfires in the Los Angeles area. The game will be played Monday night at State Farm Stadium, the home of the Arizona Cardinals, in suburban Glendale.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jazz go cold in 97-92 loss to the Heat
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) - The Utah Jazz just can't seem to find a way to be able to close out games at home this season. Tyler Herro scored 23 points and Jaime Jaquez had 20, including five in the final 1:03, to lead the Miami Heat to a 97-92 victory over the Utah Jazz on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: CMU Basketball: Men get first RMAC win under Dunlap as Women cruise past NMHU
item.description: After a three-week break, Colorado Mesa Basketball is back. And they're off to a great start in 2025.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Notre Dame QB Riley Leonard gets injury scare, returns to rally Irish into CFP championship game
item.description: MIAMI GARDENS, Fla. (AP) — Riley Leonard took a big hit and got a big scare. He wound up ending the night with the biggest win of his college career — and now, a chance at a national championship.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ohio state Sen. Ben Espy, who died at 81, to be remembered at service for breaking barriers
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Respected Ohio attorney and former state Sen. Ben Espy will be remembered at a celebration of life Monday for his decades of service to the state and its capital city. Espy died on Jan. 4 at age 81 after a brief illness.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Riley Leonard returns from injury to lead Notre Dame to national championship game in CFP win vs Penn State
item.description: Notre Dame is going to the first College Football Playoff National Championship after defeating Penn State 27-24 in the Orange Bowl on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Notre Dame forced to play backup quarterback in Orange Bowl after starter Riley Leonard leaves with injury
item.description: Notre Dame starting quarterback Riley Leonard left a College Football Playoff semifinal late in the first half and was being evaluated for an injury.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Las Vegas Raider Charles Snowden thought he was in Florida during DUI arrest: Video
item.description: Tests showed Snowden’s blood-alcohol level was more than double the legal limit after officers found him asleep and unresponsive in a running SUV, documents show.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, NewsNation

item.title: Connor Bedard continues hot stretch, career-best point streak at 8 games as Blackhawks beat Avalanche
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Connor Bedard is on a hot streak, even if the Blackhawks haven't been too hot lately. Bedard continued his roll on Wednesday night with a goal and an assist, and this time it led to a win, as the Blackhawks beat the Colorado Avalanche, 3-1, at the United Center.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Former Baltimore Orioles left-hander Brian Matusz, who made final MLB appearance with Cubs in 2016, dies at 37
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Brian Matusz, the left-handed pitcher who spent eight seasons in the major leagues and made his final big-league appearance with the Cubs during their 2016 World Series championship season, has died. He was 37. Matusz, a former No.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: St. Patrick names former Notre Dame star Tom Zbikowski head football coach
item.description: CHICAGO — One of the best safeties in Notre Dame's storied football program was named head coach of Saint Patrick High School. Tom Zbikowski, 39, will take over the Northwest Side high school's football program.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, WGN 9

item.title: Top-ranked Central girls wrestling is all about toughness and grit
item.description: Undefeated as a team to start the season, Central girls wrestling was just ranked 20th in the entire country by FloWrestling.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Giants agree to deal with Justin Verlander: reports
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- The San Francisco Giants are adding former MVP Justin Verlander to their pitching staff, multiple outlets reported Tuesday. The team reportedly agreed to a one-year deal with the pitcher. Verlander, 41, is one of the best pitchers of the 2000s and a future Hall of Famer.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: Utah State cracks the top-25 rankings
item.description: LOGAN, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - After a 14-1 start, the Utah State basketball team is back in the top-25. The Aggies check in at No. 25 in both the AP Top 25 and USA Today Coaches Poll. Utah State is 4-0 in Mountain West play, under first-year USU head coach Jerrod Calhoun.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Report: Bears seek permission to interview Cowboys' Mike McCarthy for head coaching vacancy
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — The Chicago Bears have sought permission to interview Dallas Cowboys head coach Mike McCarthy and are awaiting a response from the Cowboys, according to ESPN's Adam Schefter.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Final field for 2025 Farmers Insurance Open in San Diego shaping up
item.description: The 2025 Farmers Insurance Open is set to take place in San Diego from January 22-25, 2025, featuring a field of major championship winners, past winners, and players with San Diego ties. Tickets went on sale in October.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, Fox 5

item.title: Colts owner Jim Irsay says he's retaining GM Chris Ballard and coach Shane Steichen
item.description: About four hours after the Colts completed their season with a 26-23 overtime victory over the Jacksonville Jaguars, Irsay ended any speculation about possible offseason changes by announcing in a letter to fans that longtime general manager Chris Ballard and second-year coach Shane Steichen would r
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL MAROT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Patriots fire coach Jerod Mayo shortly after beating Bills to finish his lone season at 4-13
item.description: FOXBOROUGH, Mass. (AP) — The New England Patriots fired coach Jerod Mayo on Sunday after one season, beginning another reboot for the franchise just a year after it parted with longtime coach Bill Belichick.
item.dc:creator: KYLE HIGHTOWER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Bears walk-off the Packers to end the season 5-12
item.description: GREEN BAY, Wisc. (WGN) — For the first time in weeks, The Chicago Bears showed life in the NFL's oldest rivalry and found a way to win at the end of the ballgame. Here's how their final contest of the 2024-25 regular season played out.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Utah HC falls in overtime to Dallas, 3-2
item.description: DALLAS (AP) - Thomas Harley scored 3:01 into overtime to give the Dallas Stars their fourth straight win, 3-2 against the Utah Hockey Club at American Airlines Center on Saturday. Matt Duchene found Harley near the right post after collecting his own rebound in front of the net.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Sensabaugh pours in 34 as Jazz crush Miami, 136-100
item.description: MIAMI (AP) - Brice Sensabaugh scored a career-high 34 points, John Collins finished with 24, and the Utah Jazz snapped a five-game losing streak with a 136-100 rout over the Miami Heat on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Joe Burrow and the Cincinnati Bengals keep their playoff hopes alive by edging the Steelers 19-17
item.description: Joe Burrow passed for 277 yards and a touchdown, Cade York kicked four field goals and the Cincinnati Bengals kept their slim playoff hopes alive with a 19-17 win over the floundering Pittsburgh Steelers on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Chiefs finally depart KC for Denver amid ice storm after 4 hours spent waiting on the tarmac
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — The Chiefs finally departed from Kansas City International Airport for their game in Denver on Saturday after spending about four hours stranded on the tarmac amid an ice storm that blanketed the region ahead of an impending blizzard.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: BYU gets trounced by #14 Houston 86-55
item.description: HOUSTON (ABC4 Sports) - The BYU basketball team came into the Big 12 season thinking it could compete with the top teams in one of the toughest leagues in the nation. But if Saturday was any indication, the Cougars have a long way to go.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Red Rocks cruise past Utah State in season opener
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The fifth-ranked Utah gymnastics team kicked off its 50th anniversary season in style, overcoming a slow start to eventually secure a dominant victory over Utah State Friday night at the Huntsman Center.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Soderblom stops 38 shots as Blackhawks end 5-game skid with 4-2 win over Canadiens
item.description: Arvid Soderblom made 38 saves, Nick Foligno scored twice and the Chicago Blackhawks snapped a five-game slide with a 4-2 win over the Montreal Canadiens on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Heat suspend Jimmy Butler for 7 games and will seek to trade him
item.description: Jimmy Butler was Miami's best player for five years, the leader of a team that went to the NBA Finals twice in his tenure. He talked big and, at times, played bigger. He spoke with reverence for the city, for the franchise, for the famed “Heat Culture.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, Fox 5
